Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

It seems like the code in ptlrpc_lprocfs_svc_req_history_start() is really wrong? We're comparing signed int to unsigned int? Then shifting a signed int up to 64 bits? It seems like we ought to make all of those values __u64 rather than casting?

Looks good to me.

parents.subject: LU-6508 kunit: Replace rc2 with rc in llog_test_8

commit.subject: LU-16807 ptlrpc: fix req_history UBSAN error
commit.message:
LU-16807 ptlrpc: fix req_history UBSAN error

"UBSAN: shift-out-of-bounds" in ptlrpc_lprocfs_svc_req_history_start()
was caused by shifting a 32-bit value by up to 64 bits, depending on
the order of the CPT table (though it would always shift > 56 today).
This was hit in sanity test_417g only on ubuntu2404.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity clientdistro=ubuntu2404
Fixes: d62c7a3a19 ("LU-2360 ptlrpc: a few fixes for req_history read")
Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I5b459faddb9f1800051a0ac31f2f1075de2b116d
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/59923
Reviewed-by: Shaun Tancheff <shaun.tancheff@hpe.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>
